plan.rationale: The hypothesis suggests using GBM with roc_auc metric and holdout validation to establish a reliable baseline. The dataset size is small, so a medium quality preset with a 120-second time limit is appropriate. The class balance is reasonable, and there are no suspected leakage columns, so all features can be included.

entry.agent_rationale: The hypothesis suggests using GBM with roc_auc metric and holdout validation to establish a reliable baseline. The dataset size is small, so a medium quality preset with a 120-second time limit is appropriate. The class balance is reasonable, and there are no suspected leakage columns, so all features can be included.

plan.rationale: The hypothesis suggests using tree ensemble methods (RF and XT) to handle potential class imbalance. The f1_macro metric is chosen to better evaluate performance on imbalanced classes. A medium quality preset is used to balance performance and computational efficiency given the dataset size.

entry.agent_rationale: The hypothesis suggests using tree ensemble methods (RF and XT) to handle potential class imbalance. The f1_macro metric is chosen to better evaluate performance on imbalanced classes. A medium quality preset is used to balance performance and computational efficiency given the dataset size.

plan.rationale: The hypothesis suggests using a diverse model mix to improve model robustness and accuracy is a suitable metric for binary classification. The use of 5-fold cross-validation will help in better generalization given the moderate dataset size.
